assumptions: The boot model has exactly two abstract slots, one candidate-installation lifecycle, at most two trial attempts, atomic transitions, persistent known-good metadata, and no slot erasure/reuse, concurrency, torn write, cryptography, storage, firmware, or attacker model.

The capability model has exactly three abstract capability identifiers, two principals, two rights, one object, atomic derive/transfer/revoke transitions, no identifier reuse, and no concurrency, IPC, scheduler, memory, DMA, or persistence model.

The virtual-memory model has exactly two domains, two CPUs, two physical pages, one virtual address per domain, generations 0 through 1, and atomic map, TLB fill/evict, transfer, unmap, and shootdown-acknowledgement transitions. It has no page-table hierarchy, PCID/ASID, DMA/IOMMU, interrupt-ordering, weak-memory, concurrent page-table-write, huge-page, copy-on-write, swap, NUMA, or hardware page-walk model.

The IPC model has exactly two principals, one endpoint, two calls, two reply tokens, a one-call queue bound, endpoint epochs 0 through 1, and atomic enqueue, dispatch, cancel, timeout, reply, teardown, and reopen transitions. Call and reply-token identifiers are one-shot and never recycled. CanCall is an abstract authorization predicate, not a composed capability lookup, derivation, or revocation model. The queue is an unordered set abstraction. It has no payload, message-copy, scheduling, priority, fairness, blocking-thread, interrupt, SMP interleaving, weak-memory, resource-accounting, endpoint-transfer, kernel-object implementation, or ABI-encoding model.

The scheduler model has exactly three fixed-priority tasks, one abstract CPU context, one non-recursive lock, a preloaded low-priority critical section, one-shot task activation, atomic transitions, and a maximum of two recorded dispatch bypasses. Cancellation and timeout are modeled as blocked-to-runnable wake results that must be consumed before the task continues. Priority inheritance is immediate and transitive depth is one. The bypass bound is a safety accounting rule only: Idle remains enabled and no temporal fairness or eventual-dispatch property is checked. There is no timer clock, deadline arithmetic, quantum, multicore execution, interrupt ordering, nested lock, deadlock graph, donation chain, affinity, migration, register context, FPU/vector state, weak memory, resource quota, scheduler implementation, or ABI encoding.
TLC uses 64-bit state fingerprints; complete reachable-state exploration within the constants is not a collision-free mathematical proof.
Only state safety invariants are checked. No liveness, fairness, real-time, probabilistic, refinement, implementation-equivalence, or physical-hardware property is checked.
The hostile configurations are deliberate mutations used to demonstrate that the named invariants produce finite counterexample traces.
open_work: Model PooleFS transaction recovery and power-loss state before freezing persistent formats.
Cross-check all five current models against exact native implementation traces when those implementations exist.
Source-build and independently reproduce the model-checker and Java inputs with signed provenance, SBOM, vulnerability, license, and redistribution review.
